1.title: Islamic New Year
1.text: The first day of Muharram marks the beginning of the new Islamic (Hijri) year. It is a time for reflection, gratitude, and setting spiritual intentions for the year ahead.

2.title: Arrival at Karbala
2.text: Imam Husayn (RA) and his companions arrived at the plains of Karbala on this day in 61 AH, a prelude to the events of Ashura.

10.title: Day of Ashura
10.text: The 10th of Muharram is the Day of Ashura. It marks the day Allah saved Prophet Musa (AS) and the Israelites from Pharaoh. The Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) recommended fasting on this day, and the day before or after it.

25.title: Passing of Zayn al-Abidin
25.text: Ali ibn Husayn (RA), grandson of Ali ibn Abi Talib, was known for his exceptional devotion and supplication. His prayers are collected in the famous Sahifa al-Sajjadiyya.

1.title: First of Safar
1.text: Safar is the second month of the Islamic calendar. Pre-Islamic superstitions about this month being unlucky have no basis in Islam — every month belongs to Allah.

20.title: Arbaeen of Imam Husayn
20.text: The 40th day after the martyrdom of Imam Husayn (RA) at Karbala. Millions of Muslims mark this day with remembrance and reflection on sacrifice for justice.

28.title: Passing of the Prophet (ﷺ)
28.text: Some scholars place the passing of the Prophet (peace be upon him) on this date. It is a day of solemn remembrance of his life, character, and legacy for all of humanity.

1.title: Beginning of Rabi al-Awwal
1.text: The month of the birth of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him). Muslims around the world increase salawat (blessings upon the Prophet) throughout this blessed month.

8.title: Passing of Imam Hasan al-Askari
8.text: Imam Hasan al-Askari passed on this date. He was known for his knowledge, piety, and guidance of the Muslim community during a difficult period.

9.title: Eid Milad un-Nabi (some traditions)
9.text: Many scholars mark this date as the birthday of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him). Muslims express their love through gatherings of dhikr, recounting his seerah, and increasing salawat.

12.title: Mawlid an-Nabi
12.text: The 12th of Rabi al-Awwal is widely celebrated as the birthday of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him). It is a day to reflect on his mercy, character, and the guidance he brought to humanity.

17.title: Birth of Imam Jafar as-Sadiq
17.text: Imam Jafar as-Sadiq was born on this day. He was a renowned scholar of Islamic jurisprudence, theology, and science, and a teacher to many great scholars.

1.title: Beginning of Rabi al-Thani
1.text: The fourth month of the Islamic calendar. Continue increasing good deeds and dhikr throughout the year.

11.title: Passing of Abdul Qadir Gilani
11.text: Sheikh Abdul Qadir Gilani (RA), the great scholar and saint of Baghdad, passed on this day. He is remembered for his profound spiritual teachings and revival of Islamic knowledge.

1.title: Beginning of Jumada al-Ula
1.text: The fifth month of the Islamic calendar. A time to remain steadfast in worship and remembrance of Allah.

5.title: Battle of Mu'tah
5.text: The Battle of Mu'tah in 8 AH was a significant military engagement between Muslim forces and the Byzantine Empire. Zayd ibn Harithah, Jafar ibn Abi Talib, and Abdullah ibn Rawahah (RA) were all martyred.

13.title: Passing of Fatimah az-Zahra
13.text: Fatimah (RA), the beloved daughter of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him), passed away in 11 AH. She is remembered as one of the greatest women in Islam.

1.title: Beginning of Jumada al-Thani
1.text: The sixth month of the Islamic calendar. Stay consistent in your daily prayers and Quran recitation.

20.title: Birth of Fatimah az-Zahra
20.text: Some scholars mark this date as the birth of Fatimah (RA), daughter of the Prophet (peace be upon him). She is a role model of faith, patience, and devotion.

1.title: Beginning of Rajab
1.text: Rajab is one of the four sacred months in Islam (alongside Dhul Qi'dah, Dhul Hijjah, and Muharram). Extra worship and seeking forgiveness are especially encouraged.

13.title: Birth of Ali ibn Abi Talib
13.text: Ali ibn Abi Talib (RA), the cousin and son-in-law of the Prophet (peace be upon him), was born inside the Kaaba. He was known for his bravery, justice, and deep knowledge of Islam.

27.title: Isra and Mi'raj
27.text: The 27th of Rajab commemorates the Night Journey (Isra) of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) from Makkah to Jerusalem, and his Ascension (Mi'raj) through the heavens. During this miraculous journey, the five daily prayers were ordained.

1.title: Beginning of Sha'ban
1.text: Sha'ban is the month before Ramadan. The Prophet (peace be upon him) used to fast frequently during this month. It is an ideal time to prepare spiritually for Ramadan.

3.title: Birth of Imam Husayn
3.text: Imam Husayn (RA), the grandson of the Prophet (peace be upon him), was born on this day in 4 AH. He is remembered for his ultimate sacrifice for truth and justice at Karbala.

15.title: Shab-e-Barat (Night of Forgiveness)
15.text: The 15th night of Sha'ban is observed by many Muslims as a night of forgiveness and mercy. It is recommended to seek Allah's forgiveness, visit graves, and increase in prayer and supplication.

1.title: First Day of Ramadan
1.text: The blessed month of fasting begins. Muslims abstain from food, drink, and other desires from dawn to sunset, devoting themselves to prayer, Quran recitation, and acts of charity.

10.title: Passing of Khadijah (RA)
10.text: Khadijah bint Khuwaylid (RA), the first wife of the Prophet (peace be upon him) and the first person to accept Islam, passed away during Ramadan. She is remembered for her unwavering faith, support, and love.

17.title: Battle of Badr
17.text: The Battle of Badr in 2 AH was the first major military victory for the Muslim community. Despite being outnumbered three to one, the Muslims were victorious, affirming Allah’s promise to support the believers.

20.title: Conquest of Makkah
20.text: In 8 AH, the Prophet (peace be upon him) entered Makkah peacefully with 10,000 companions. He forgave the Quraysh, cleansed the Kaaba of idols, and declared: "Truth has come and falsehood has vanished."

21.title: Last Ten Nights Begin
21.text: The last ten nights of Ramadan are the most spiritually intense period of the year. The Prophet (peace be upon him) would increase his worship, seek Laylat al-Qadr, and perform i'tikaf (spiritual retreat) in the masjid.

27.title: Laylat al-Qadr
27.text: The Night of Power — better than a thousand months (Quran 97:3). While its exact date is unknown, the 27th night is most commonly observed. Increase in prayer, Quran recitation, and the dua: "Allahumma innaka afuwwun tuhibbul afwa fa’fu anni."

1.title: Eid al-Fitr
1.text: The Festival of Breaking the Fast. After a month of fasting, Muslims celebrate with the Eid prayer, giving Zakat al-Fitr, visiting family, and sharing meals. It is a day of gratitude, joy, and community.

2.title: Six Fasts of Shawwal
2.text: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: "Whoever fasts Ramadan and follows it with six days of Shawwal, it is as if they fasted the entire year" (Muslim). These six days can be fasted anytime during Shawwal.

1.title: Beginning of Dhul Qi'dah
1.text: Dhul Qi'dah is one of the four sacred months. Warfare was traditionally prohibited during this month. It is a time to increase in worship as the Hajj season approaches.

25.title: Dahw al-Ard
25.text: Some traditions mark this day as when Allah spread the earth from beneath the Kaaba. It is a day of reflection on creation and the majesty of Allah.

1.title: Beginning of Dhul Hijjah
1.text: The month of Hajj begins. The first ten days of Dhul Hijjah are among the most blessed days of the year. The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: "There are no days in which righteous deeds are more beloved to Allah than these ten days" (Bukhari).

8.title: Day of Tarwiyah
8.text: The 8th of Dhul Hijjah marks the beginning of the Hajj rituals. Pilgrims head to Mina, where they spend the day and night in prayer and preparation for the Day of Arafah.

9.title: Day of Arafah
9.text: Standing at Arafah is the central pillar of Hajj. For those not performing Hajj, fasting on this day expiates the sins of the previous year and the coming year (Muslim). It is a day of immense mercy and forgiveness.

10.title: Eid al-Adha
10.text: The Festival of Sacrifice commemorates Prophet Ibrahim's (AS) willingness to sacrifice his son in obedience to Allah. Muslims perform the Eid prayer, sacrifice an animal (Qurbani), and distribute the meat to family, friends, and those in need.

11.title: Days of Tashreeq (Day 1)
11.text: The 11th, 12th, and 13th of Dhul Hijjah are the Days of Tashreeq — days of eating, drinking, and remembering Allah. The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: "The Days of Tashreeq are days of eating, drinking, and dhikr of Allah."

12.title: Days of Tashreeq (Day 2)
12.text: The second of the three Days of Tashreeq. Continue the Takbeer (Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar, La ilaha illallah, Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar, wa lillahil hamd) after every prayer.

13.title: Days of Tashreeq (Day 3)
13.text: The final Day of Tashreeq. Pilgrims completing Hajj stone the Jamarat for the last time and prepare to leave Mina. A day to complete acts of charity and gratitude.

18.title: Eid al-Ghadir
18.text: On this day in 10 AH, at Ghadir Khumm, the Prophet (peace be upon him) delivered his final sermon after the Farewell Hajj. Many Muslims commemorate this event and its significance for the Muslim community.
